title: OOF empirical stop-percentile priors by track and compound
summary: Official strategy guides emphasize that degradation level and pit-lane time change whether a one-stop or two-stop is viable.
rationale: Official strategy guides emphasize that degradation level and pit-lane time change whether a one-stop or two-stop is viable. That implies the useful signal is not a universal compound life constant, but how unusual the current tyre age/lap is for this circuit-strategy context.
implementation_hint: Build out-of-fold smoothed historical priors from train only: empirical CDF/hazard of stopping at current `TyreLife`, `LapNumber`, or `RaceProgress` for backoff groups like `(Race, Compound, Stint)`, `(Race, Compound)`, `(Compound, Stint)`, and global `(Compound)`. Emit `stop_percentile`, `hazard_now`, `distance_to_group_median_stop`, and `delta_vs_global_compound_prior`.
expected_effect: Medium upside by replacing brittle fixed windows with track-aware, data-driven stop-pressure features.
risk: Largest validation trap in this set: any in-fold aggregation or use of future seasons/races will inflate CV. Sparse groups need aggressive shrinkage/backoff.
